market_context: Market sentiment on X/Twitter is cautiously optimistic, with many users discussing potential rate cuts and tech sector strength. Pre-market futures for S&P 500 and Nasdaq are up slightly by 0.3% and 0.4% respectively, while notable movers include tech giants like AAPL and NVDA. No major economic data releases are scheduled, but a Fed speech later today could influence sentiment.

predictions.reasoning: Positive pre-market futures for S&P 500 (+0.3%) suggest upward momentum. X/Twitter sentiment is leaning bullish with discussions around potential Fed dovishness. However, uncertainty around the Fed speech tempers confidence.

NVDA is a notable pre-market mover, up 1.2%, driven by recent AI sector hype on X/Twitter. Web searches indicate strong options flow with heavy call buying for March expirations. Confidence is moderate due to potential volatility from broader market reactions.

AAPL shows pre-market strength (+0.5%) alongside positive mentions on X/Twitter regarding new product rumors. Web data suggests steady institutional buying, though short interest remains a risk factor. Confidence is limited due to lack of concrete news catalysts.
